config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikalahkan begitu saja oleh klaim “kepentingan masyarakat”, karena justru di situlah sering muncul penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan membatasi hak seperlunya, bukan meniadakan hak orang per orang. Kalau tidak, atas nama masyarakat, suara minoritas dan kebebasan warga bisa mudah diinjak.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective in the United States, that idea is dangerous because it can turn into a blank check for government or the majority to trample minority rights in the name of the “greater good.” At the same time, individual rights are not absolute in every situation, since public safety and basic social order do matter, but any limit on rights should be narrow, justified, and closely watched. If society always wins by default, then the whole point of constitutional rights is weakened.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat politik dan birokrasi di Indonesia, dalih “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, bahkan kadang merugikan kelompok kecil atau orang yang tidak punya suara. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi justru harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar agar mayoritas tidak semena-mena. Jadi, menurut saya, yang benar adalah menyeimbangkan keduanya, bukan menempatkan masyarakat selalu di atas individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take blanket priority over the rights of the individual. From an American perspective, I am wary of any argument that gives the majority or the state too much power, because history shows how easily "the common good" can become an excuse for censorship, discrimination, or abuse. That said, I do not believe individual rights are absolute in every case; public safety, due process, and basic social order can justify limited restrictions when they are narrowly tailored and accountable. The real test is whether society is protecting people without turning constitutional rights into something optional.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak keras gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk menutup kritik, menekan minoritas, atau membenarkan kebijakan yang sebenarnya tidak adil, jadi saya sangat waspada terhadap logika seperti itu. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi justru harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar agar negara dan mayoritas tidak semena-mena. Yang masuk akal adalah keseimbangan yang ketat: hak individu tetap jadi pegangan utama, sementara pembatasan hanya boleh dilakukan secara terbatas, jelas alasannya, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take blanket priority over the rights of the individual. From an American perspective, I am very suspicious of any rule that lets the majority or the state override rights whenever it claims to be acting for the common good, because that is exactly how abuse, censorship, and discrimination get normalized. I do think society can justify limited restrictions in cases like public safety or due process, but those limits have to be narrow, clearly explained, and accountable. If individual rights are treated as optional, then they stop meaning much at all.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
